echo "=== Brisbane International 2026 final (women's) ==="
for d in 03 04 05; do
  f="articles/2026/01/$d/articles.jsonl"
  [ -f "$f" ] && grep -ohiE "[A-Za-z ]+ (beat|defeats|wins|beats) [A-Za-z ]+(final|Brisbane)?" "$f" 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "brisbane|sabalenka|rybakina|polini" | head -6
  [ -f "$f" ] && grep -ohiE "brisbane[^.]*(final|sabalenka|rybakina)[^.]*" "$f" 2>/dev/null | head -5
done
echo "=== Senegal v Mali QF (Jan 9) ==="
for d in 08 09; do
  f="articles/2026/01/$d/articles.jsonl"
  [ -f "$f" ] && grep -ohiE "(senegal|mali)[^.]{0,80}(quarter|semi|win|beat|advance)[^.]*" "$f" 2>/dev/null | head -6
done

HEADLINE: Elena Rybakina wins Brisbane International after seeing off Aryna Sabalenka
SOURCE: www.independent.co.uk
PUBLISHED: 2024-01-07 00:00:00 | DOWNLOADED: 2024-01-07 00:00:00
URL: https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/tennis/elena-rybakina-brisbane-international-aryna-sabalenka-brisbane-kazakhstan-b2474447.html

Elena Rybakina wins Brisbane International after seeing off Aryna Sabalenka

Elena Rybakina defeated world number two Aryna Sabalenka in their Australian Open final rematch with a dominant straight sets win to claim the Brisbane International. The world number four from Kazakhstan charged out of the blocks, dominating the first set 6-0 and continuing the form through the second set 6-3, winning in just over 70 minutes. Rybakina was clinical on break points, winning five of a possible seven, and limiting the Belarusian to just one break point. Sabalenka was on a 15-game winning streak in Australia before the loss, spanning back to the start of 2023 in Adelaide and continuing through her Australian Open-winning campaign. The win was 24-year-old Rybakina’s sixth career title.
